candidate_id.description: Id of the candidate this note is attached to. Notes are always anchored to a candidate; `application_id` is an optional second anchor for application-scoped notes.

application_id.description: Id of the application this note is attached to, when the note was authored against a specific application (for example a stage transition or a feedback note). `null` for candidate-level notes that are not tied to any single application.

body.description: Free-text body of the note. `@`-mentions are rendered as plain user names; use `body_with_tags` for the version that preserves mention markup. Newline characters are stored verbatim and rendered as line breaks in the Greenhouse UI.

subject.description: Subject line on the note. Always populated for `EMAIL` notes; usually `null` for other types.

type.description: Note classification. `NOTE` and `EMAIL` are user-authored candidate notes and logged emails; `ACTIVITY` covers system-generated activity feed entries plus partner-logged activities. Other values (`INTERVIEW`, `FEEDBACK`, `TOUCHPOINT`, `FOLLOW_UP`, `TAKE_HOME_TEST`, `LINKEDIN_NOTE`, `LINKEDIN_INMAIL`, `AVAILABILITY_REQUEST`, `FORM`, `MIGRATION_ERROR`) identify notes produced by specific Greenhouse features and are read-only via this API.

user_id.description: Id of the Greenhouse user who authored the note. `null` for notes generated by the system rather than a specific user.

email_from.description: Comma-separated list of `From` addresses on the logged email. Only populated when `type` is `EMAIL`.

email_to.description: Comma-separated list of `To` addresses on the logged email. Only populated when `type` is `EMAIL`.

email_cc.description: `Cc` addresses on the logged email, one entry per recipient. Only populated when `type` is `EMAIL`.

import_hash.description: Stable de-duplication key set when the note was created by a bulk import or migration. `null` for notes authored through the UI or Harvest. Use it to detect re-runs of the same import.

body_with_tags.description: `body` with `@`-mentions rendered as embedded user tags (e.g. `@[User Name](user:123)`) instead of plain user names, for partners that need to resolve mentioned users.

visibility.description: Who can see this note. `publicly_visible` is visible to anyone with access to the candidate, `privately_visible` is restricted to users with the "see private notes" permission, and `admin_only_visible` is restricted to Job Admins and Site Admins. Defaults to `admin_only_visible` on create.

description: Schema for the 'notes' stream, linked to the '/v3/notes' endpoint.
